skipped_files.reason: JSON data file — not a command/agent markdown documentation file. Prompt verbosity detection rules apply only to .md files under commands/ and agents/ directories. No verbose sections detectable.

JSON artifact/report file — not a command/agent markdown documentation file. Prompt verbosity detection rules apply only to .md files under commands/ and agents/ directories. No verbose sections detectable.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: CODEX_FEEDBACK: All four challenge points returned OBSERVATION_ONLY with no PROPOSED_FIX items. (1) No false positive — JSON files are correctly excluded from verbosity scope. (2) Broader interpretation does not apply — JSON string fields are functional metadata/audit trail, not markdown narrative sections. (3) Severity thresholds not triggered — verbose_lines=0 for both files; thresholds (200/100/50) are not reached. (4) Scope determination is correct — prompt-inspector scope is commands/*.md and agents/*.md; these .json files under tests/generated/ and docs/dev/ are out of scope. Codex noted it would be cleaner to record these as skipped/out-of-scope rather than 'inspected with no violation', which is reflected in this report via the skipped_files field.

findings.issue: JSON files should not be flagged for verbosity — false positive check
findings.rationale: Draft NO VIOLATION is correct. Both files are structured JSON artifacts, not command/agent markdown prompt files.

findings.issue: Broader interpretation of verbose content in JSON strings
findings.rationale: JSON string values are functional manifest/report fields (test metadata, verification notes, diff stats, audit trail) — not markdown narrative sections like ## Philosophy or ## Overview.
Thresholds apply to verbose_lines count, not total file length. verbose_lines=0 for both files; no severity classification applies.
Scope is correctly limited to commands/*.md and agents/*.md. JSON files under tests/generated/ and docs/dev/ are out of scope.
Codex suggested recording as skipped/out-of-scope for clarity. Applied: report uses skipped_files field rather than findings with zero violations.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex observation about skipped/out-of-scope presentation applied: added skipped_files[] field to report output. All other findings were OBSERVATION_ONLY with no PROPOSED_FIX — logged only, no changes required to findings verdict.
